title: Sphere Packing — Proof DAG (Cohn–Elkies linear programming, Viazovska's magic functions in $E_8$ and Leech, Radchenko–Viazovska Fourier interpolation, universal optimality, and the Lean formalization)
description: The proof that the $E_8$ and Leech lattices realize the densest sphere packings in dimensions 8 and 24. Two pieces fit together: the Cohn–Elkies linear-programming bound (`math/0110009`) reduces the problem to constructing a Schwartz function whose Fourier transform satisfies sign/value constraints at lattice radii — the `magic function`; and Viazovska's modular-form construction (`1603.04246` for $E_8$; `1603.06518` for Leech, extending the same template) produces such a function from Eisenstein-series quasi-modular forms with the right interpolation profile. The Cohn–Miller paper (`1603.04759`) studies the optimal functions structurally; Radchenko–Viazovska (`1701.00265`) abstracts the construction into a general Fourier-interpolation framework on the real line; CKMRV (`1902.05438`) lifts the framework to all symmetric powers and proves universal optimality of $E_8$ and Leech for completely monotonic energies, with the earlier Cohn–Kumar paper (`math/0607446`) supplying the universal-optimality formalism on spheres. Hariharan–Birkbeck–Lee (`2604.23468`) formalize Viazovska's $E_8$ proof in Lean — the dimension-8 sphere-packing Lean blueprint, which serves as external ground truth for the classical-core layer of this DAG (mirroring the role of PNT+ for prime-number-theorem and the PFR blueprint for pfr).

math/0110009.title: New upper bounds on sphere packings I
math/0110009.role: foundation — the Cohn–Elkies linear-programming bound: a sphere-packing density upper bound from any radial Schwartz function $f$ with $f(0)=\hat f(0)$, $f \le 0$ outside a ball of radius $r$, $\hat f \ge 0$. Reduces the geometric problem to a function-construction problem (`magic function`).

1603.04246.title: The sphere packing problem in dimension 8
1603.04246.role: headline — constructs the magic function for $E_8$ from Eisenstein series and quasi-modular forms, proving $E_8$ is the densest sphere packing in $\mathbb{R}^8$. The Fields-medal result.

1603.06518.title: The sphere packing problem in dimension 24
1603.06518.role: Leech-lattice analog — extends Viazovska's $E_8$ magic-function template to the Leech lattice, proving its optimality in dimension 24.

1603.04759.title: Some properties of optimal functions for sphere packing in dimensions 8 and 24
1603.04759.role: structural companion — analyzes the magic functions Viazovska/CKMRV constructed: uniqueness, eigenfunction structure, modular-form interpretation. The bridge between the existence proofs and the universal-optimality framework.

1701.00265.title: Fourier interpolation on the real line
1701.00265.role: abstracted framework — proves a Schwartz function on $\mathbb{R}$ is uniquely determined by $f(\pm\sqrt n)$ and $\hat f(\pm\sqrt n)$ for $n\ge 0$; the analytic skeleton behind the magic functions, with explicit interpolation basis from modular forms.

1902.05438.title: Universal optimality of the $E_8$ and Leech lattices and interpolation formulas
1902.05438.role: sequel — proves $E_8$ and Leech are universally optimal: minimizers of every completely monotonic pairwise energy. Generalizes the Fourier-interpolation framework to higher-dimensional spheres and to non-Gaussian potentials.

math/0607446.title: Universally optimal distribution of points on spheres
math/0607446.role: universal-optimality formalism — develops the framework of universal optimality for finite point configurations on spheres via linear-programming duality, the precursor used and extended by 1902.05438.

2604.23468.title: A Milestone in Formalization: The Sphere Packing Problem in Dimension 8
2604.23468.role: Lean formalization (blueprint anchor) — formalizes Viazovska's $E_8$ proof in Lean 4. The dimension-8 sphere-packing Lean blueprint that serves as external ground truth for this DAG's classical-core layer, paralleling the validation role of the PFR blueprint and PNT+ for the other indexed programs.

Hales:2005.title: T. C. Hales — A proof of the Kepler conjecture (densest packing in $\mathbb{R}^3$)
Hales:2005.role: foundational — the dimension-3 sphere-packing theorem (Kepler conjecture), proved by Hales via computer-assisted optimization. Not part of the Cohn–Elkies / Viazovska linear-programming program but the dimension-3 analogue of the same problem.

ConwaySloane:SPLAG.title: Conway–Sloane — Sphere Packings, Lattices and Groups
ConwaySloane:SPLAG.role: foundational — the standard reference for the $E_8$, Leech, and other extremal lattices and their packing densities.

Mazur-Ribet-Wiles:ModularForms.title: Modular forms / Eisenstein series — classical theory
Mazur-Ribet-Wiles:ModularForms.role: foundational — the modular-forms machinery (Eisenstein series of weight $k$, quasi-modular forms, theta series of lattices) Viazovska's magic function is built from.

blueprint_anchor.project: Sphere-Packing-Lean (originally kickstarted at EPFL in March 2024 by M. Viazovska and S. Hariharan; completed February 2026)
blueprint_anchor.maintainers: Christopher Birkbeck, Sidharth Hariharan, Bhavik Mehta, Seewoo Lee
blueprint_anchor.ai_assist: The final stages of the dimension-8 verification (Feb 2026) were completed with assistance from Math, Inc.'s 'Gauss' autoformalization model — directly relevant to AI for Math: the formalization the blueprint anchors itself was partly produced by an AI autoformalization system, an example of the kind of human–AI workflow this DAG infrastructure is meant to support.
blueprint_anchor.blueprint_size: 139 nodes (19 theorems, 17 propositions, 15 corollaries, 54 lemmas, 34 definitions) — verified by fetching the live blueprint dep-graph on 2026-05-26
blueprint_anchor.role: External ground truth for the classical-core layer (Viazovska's $E_8$ proof). The blueprint's statement-level dependency graph is the precision/recall yardstick for our extracted nodes and edges on 1603.04246 — the third such blueprint anchor in the registry, after PFR (`pfr` DAG) and PNT+ (`prime-number-theorem` DAG). Unique here in two ways: (i) the formalization paper itself (`2604.23468`) is an arXiv node in this DAG, so the link between informal proof and formal verification is captured directly as an intra-DAG `imports_result` edge rather than only via external reference; (ii) the formalization itself was partly AI-assisted (Math, Inc. Gauss), making this the registry's clearest example of the human–AI–graph workflow the proposal envisions.
blueprint_anchor.verified_blueprint_matches: The blueprint's Cohn–Elkies LP bound (Theorem 5.1 / Theorem 5.2, explicitly titled 'Cohn–Elkies') corresponds verbatim to math/0110009 Theorem 2 and to 1603.04246 Theorem 2. A full label-by-label correspondence table for the remaining Viazovska nodes against Chapter 6 / Chapter 7 of the blueprint has not yet been built and would require an expert pass — flagged as an open audit item, analogous to the open PFR 16-node table.
